flows.name: User Authentication (BlazorAdmin)

steps.operation: GetTokenAsync(username) — creates JWT with user claims and roles, 7-day expiry
UserManager.FindByNameAsync, GetRolesAsync — reads AspNetUsers and AspNetUserRoles tables
Sets HttpClient.DefaultRequestHeaders.Authorization = Bearer {token}; notifies Blazor auth state changed

risk_flags: JWT_SECRET_KEY is hardcoded in source (AuthorizationConstants.JWT_SECRET_KEY) — plaintext secret in repository
7-day JWT expiry with no refresh mechanism visible — long-lived tokens increase security risk
open_questions: How does the Web (MVC) project authenticate? Does it use cookie-based auth separately from this JWT endpoint?

flows.name: Admin Lists Catalog Items (BlazorAdmin)

steps.operation: List() — checks localStorage for 'items' key; if fresh (< 1 min old) returns cached list
List() — fires 3 parallel Tasks: brandListTask, typeListTask, itemListTask via HttpService
GET api/catalog-items — returns PagedCatalogItemResponse (source not in Layer 1)
Joins brand/type names onto catalog items after all tasks complete
Stores result in localStorage for 1-minute cache
CallRequestRefresh() — triggers UI re-render via RefreshBroadcast singleton

external_systems_touched: SQL Server (Catalog DB — Catalog table, CatalogBrands, CatalogTypes)
risk_flags: Browser localStorage used as cache — cache is per-browser tab, not shared; multiple tabs can have stale data
CatalogItemService always fires 3 parallel HTTP calls regardless of which data is needed — no lazy loading
CatalogItemListEndpoint implementation unknown — cannot verify pagination, filtering, or authorization
open_questions: Is the catalog-items list endpoint paginated? CatalogItemService.ListPaged uses ?PageSize=10 but List() has no pagination parameter.
Is the admin UI protected by [Authorize(Roles=Administrators)]? Authorization enforcement on API side is unknown.

flows.name: Add Item to Basket
flows.entry_point: unknown — Web project basket controller (POST /basket/add-item inferred)

steps.operation: Receives basketId (or username), catalogItemId, price, quantity
SELECT Baskets WHERE BuyerId = username INCLUDE BasketItems
AddItem(catalogItemId, price, quantity) — either adds new BasketItem or increments existing
UpdateAsync(basket) — persists updated basket with items

external_systems_touched: SQL Server (Catalog DB — Baskets, BasketItems tables)
risk_flags: Web basket controller source not available — entry point and authorization unknown
open_questions: How is the price passed to AddItemToBasket? Is it taken from the catalog item at add-time or from the request? If from request, there is a price manipulation risk.
Is there a maximum basket quantity enforced?

flows.name: Checkout — Place Order

steps.operation: ListAsync(CatalogItemsSpecification(catalogItemIds)) — fetches catalog items for price snapshot
SELECT from Catalog WHERE Id IN (...) — bulk fetch
ComposePicUri(catalogItem.PictureUri) — replaces placeholder URL with actual base URL
new Order(buyerId, shippingAddress, items) — creates order aggregate with items as OrderItem snapshots

external_systems_touched: SQL Server (Catalog DB — Orders, OrderItems, Baskets, Catalog tables)
risk_flags: Basket is NOT deleted after order creation — no explicit basket cleanup in OrderService.CreateOrderAsync
All reads and writes occur in the same CatalogContext — no transaction scope defined explicitly; relies on EF Core implicit transaction
Price at order time uses catalogItem.Price from DB — correct snapshot behavior, but if catalog price changes between add-to-basket and checkout, the basket UnitPrice may differ from current DB price
open_questions: Is the basket deleted/cleared after successful order creation? Not visible in OrderService.
Is there payment processing? PaymentMethod entity exists in Buyer aggregate but is not referenced anywhere in visible code.
Is there any inventory/stock tracking? Not visible in the codebase artifacts.

flows.name: Anonymous Basket Transfer on Login
flows.entry_point: IBasketService.TransferBasketAsync (called on login — Web project event handler inferred)

steps.operation: User logs in — triggers basket transfer for anonymous session
If no anonymous basket exists, returns early. Otherwise finds or creates user basket.
AddItem for each anonymous basket item into user basket

risk_flags: If user basket already has items AND anonymous basket has same items, AddItem will increment quantity — potential unintended quantity doubling
Transfer happens in two separate UpdateAsync + DeleteAsync calls — no explicit transaction; partial failure possible
open_questions: What triggers TransferBasketAsync? Login event in Web? Cookie event? Not visible in Layer 1 artifacts.

flows.name: Admin Creates Catalog Item (BlazorAdmin)

steps.operation: POST api/catalog-items — validates, creates CatalogItem entity
RefreshLocalStorageList() — clears and reloads localStorage cache

risk_flags: Create endpoint source not in Layer 1 — authorization (Administrators role required?) is unknown
Image upload via base64 in CreateCatalogItemRequest — large images could cause request size issues
open_questions: How is the picture stored? CreateCatalogItemRequest has PictureBase64 and PictureName fields — is there a file upload endpoint?
Is the create endpoint protected by [Authorize(Roles=Administrators)]?
